# GitHub Labels Guide

This document describes the labels used in this repository to organize issues and pull requests.

## How to Use Labels

Labels help organize and prioritize work. When creating an issue or pull request, select the appropriate label(s) to categorize your contribution.

## Label Categories

### Type Labels
- **`bug`** - Something isn't working correctly
- **`enhancement`** - New feature or request
- **`documentation`** - Improvements or additions to documentation
- **`refactor`** - Code improvements without feature changes
- **`chore`** - Maintenance tasks, dependencies, etc.

### Priority Labels
- **`priority: critical`** - Urgent, needs immediate attention
- **`priority: high`** - Important, should be addressed soon
- **`priority: medium`** - Standard priority
- **`priority: low`** - Nice to have, can wait

### Status Labels
- **`in progress`** - Currently being worked on
- **`blocked`** - Cannot proceed until something else is done
- **`needs review`** - Ready for code review
- **`on hold`** - Paused temporarily

### Difficulty Labels
- **`good first issue`** - Great for newcomers/beginners
- **`help wanted`** - Community contribution welcome
- **`intermediate`** - Moderate difficulty
- **`advanced`** - High difficulty

### Other Labels
- **`question`** - Question or discussion
- **`duplicate`** - This issue or PR already exists
- **`invalid`** - This doesn't seem right
- **`wontfix`** - Won't be implemented

## How to Create Labels

1. Go to your repository
2. Click **Issues** → **Labels**
3. Click **New Label**
4. Enter the label name, description, and color
5. Click **Create Label**

## Best Practices

- Use labels consistently across all issues and PRs
- Combine multiple labels for better organization (e.g., `bug` + `priority: high`)
- Remove labels once they're no longer relevant
- Use `good first issue` to welcome new contributors

---

For more information, see [GitHub's label documentation](https://docs.github.com/en/issues/using-labels-and-milestones-to-track-work/managing-labels).
